items.final_wording: Supports accurate, complete, and legible anesthesia documentation across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.

source.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care
source.population: Patients receiving anesthesia services across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.
source.setting: Anesthesia care continuum and anesthesia medical records.
source.exclusions: Exact for documentation fields and continuity of anesthesia records. It supports recording clinician-assessed facts and completed plans, not generating management, medicines, doses, procedures, referrals, discharge, or urgency.

exact_location.heading: Documentation across the anesthesia care continuum

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports accurate, complete, and legible anesthesia documentation across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.

items.rationale: The ASA statement supports complete documentation across anesthesia-provider procedural and monitored-anesthesia care without supplying clinical content.

items.final_wording: Supports documented oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain or other recovery observations, and use of a recovery score where appropriate.

source.title: Standards for Postanesthesia Care
source.population: Patients who receive general anesthesia, regional anesthesia, or monitored anesthesia care.
source.setting: PACU or equivalent postanesthesia recovery location.
source.exclusions: Exact for transfer, arrival evaluation, handover, recovery monitoring and records, discharge criteria, and qualified discharge responsibility. It does not generate discharge or treatment decisions.

exact_location.heading: Standard III — continual evaluation and recovery record

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documented oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain or other recovery observations, and use of a recovery score where appropriate.

items.rationale: The current ASA standard supports documenting oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain, and other recovery observations without generating findings or treatment.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.

source.title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines on sedation in gastrointestinal endoscopy
source.population: People undergoing gastrointestinal endoscopy with recommendation-specific sedation, procedure, age, and comorbidity contexts.
source.setting: Endoscopy pre-assessment, procedure, recovery, and discharge.
source.exclusions: Exact only for selected procedure-context, monitored-recovery, discharge-assessment, follow-up-planning, and safety-instruction documentation. It does not support retrospective diagnosis, pathology interpretation, treatment, or universal post-endoscopy follow-up.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.

items.rationale: The BSG section supports recovery and information-exchange documentation only for gastrointestinal endoscopy sedation.

items.final_wording: Supports re-evaluation, equipment, medicines, staff, monitoring, techniques, positioning, lines and procedures, unusual events, fluids or blood products, output, and transfer status as recorded facts.

source.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care
source.population: Patients receiving anesthesia services across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.
source.setting: Anesthesia care continuum and anesthesia medical records.
source.exclusions: Exact for documentation fields and continuity of anesthesia records. It supports recording clinician-assessed facts and completed plans, not generating management, medicines, doses, procedures, referrals, discharge, or urgency.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports re-evaluation, equipment, medicines, staff, monitoring, techniques, positioning, lines and procedures, unusual events, fluids or blood products, output, and transfer status as recorded facts.

items.rationale: The intraoperative documentation section was reviewed for staff, monitoring, medicines, technique, and events, but no corresponding specific legacy item existed for mapping.
